There is **only one existing `quarkus-extension.yaml`** file in the repository at the base commit:

**File:** `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml`

**Full contents:**
```yaml
name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server
description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily.
metadata:
  keywords:
    - mcp-server
#  guide: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/mcp-server/dev/ # To create and publish this guide, see https://github.com/quarkiverse/quarkiverse/wiki#documenting-your-extension
  categories:
    - "miscellaneous"
  status: "preview"
```

**Conventions observed:**
- **`name`**: Full human-readable name of the extension
- **`description`**: Brief summary sentence at the **top level** (not under `metadata`)
- **`metadata.keywords`**: list of relevant keywords (e.g., `mcp-server`)
- **`metadata.guide`**: URL to documentation guide (commented out in the existing file)
- **`metadata.categories`**: list of category strings (e.g., `"miscellaneous"`)
- **`metadata.status`**: lifecycle status (e.g., `"preview"`)

**Notable difference from the PR's new files:** The existing file includes a top-level `description` field, whereas the two new files added by this PR (`transports/sse/runtime/...` and `transports/stdio/runtime/...`) do **not** include a `description` field — only a `name` and `metadata` block.

**Finding:**

There are **no existing `<description>` elements** in any `pom.xml` file at the base commit. Across all 15 pom.xml files (root, core, transports/sse, transports/stdio, test-utils, docs, samples), none contain a `<description>` tag. This PR introduces `<description>` elements for the first time to `transports/sse/pom.xml` and `transports/stdio/pom.xml`.

For `quarkus-extension.yaml` conventions, there is **one existing file** at `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml`:
```yaml
name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server
description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily.
metadata:
  keywords:
    - mcp-server
  categories:
    - "miscellaneous"
  status: "preview"
```

**Notable difference:** The PR's new `quarkus-extension.yaml` files for SSE and stdio **omit the `description` field** that the core extension has, while they do include a `guide` URL (which core has commented out).

<review>
SUMMARY:
This PR adds metadata (descriptions and quarkus-extension.yaml files) to the SSE and stdio transport extensions so they display properly on code.quarkus and Maven Central. Two issues were identified: a missing `description` field that breaks consistency with the core extension, and a typo in the stdio pom.xml description.

POINTS:
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:1] and [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:1] The core extension at `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml` includes a top-level `description` field (`"This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily."`), but the two new files omit it. Consider adding a `description` field to each (e.g., `"This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using server side events (SSE)."` and similar for stdio) to maintain consistency with the existing convention.
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] Typo: the added `<description>` reads `"stanard input/output (stdio)"` — should be `"standard"` instead of `"stanard"`.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:5] and [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:5] The `guide` URL (`https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html`) is uncommented here, while the core extension has its guide URL commented out with a note about publishing. Please confirm this URL is live; if not, consider commenting it out like the core extension does.
</review>
